data.title: LiveOne exceeds 1.3M subscribers and ad-supported users
data.description:
• LiveOne (NASDAQ:LVO) a music, entertainment, and technology platform said on Thursday that it has surpassed 1.3M subscribers and ad-supported users, a 130% increase since January 1, 2025.
  • The company also unveiled multi-tier revenue drivers leveraging a 12-person sales team, DAX partnership, and AI marketing and Tesla partnership to convert to paid subscribers.

data.title: RWE AG GAAP EPS of €3.12, revenue of €24.22B
data.description:
• RWE AG press release (OTCPK:RWEOY): FY GAAP EPS of €3.12.
  • Revenue of €24.22B (-15.2% Y/Y).

data.title: Initial jobless claims rise slightly in past week, in line with expectations
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for week ended March 15: +2K to 223K vs. 224K consensus and 221K prior (revised from 220K), according to data released by the U.S. Department of Labor on Thursday.

Continuing claims: 1.892M vs. 1.890M consensus and 1.859M prior (revised from 1.870M).

data.title: Microchip enlists Macquarie to help sale Arizona plant
data.description:
• Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) said on Thursday that it has enlisted investment firm Macquarie Group to help sell its wafer fabrication plant in Tempe, Arizona.
  • Shares fell nearly 5% in premarket trading, due in part to the company filing a mixed shelf securities on Wednesday.
  • The decision is part of Microchip's previously announced restructuring plan to help boost efficiency and profitability. Included in the potential sale is the Fab 2 site, with all manufacturing and technologies inside the plant being transferred to its Oregon and Colorado fabs. 
  • “The closure and sale of Fab 2 is the latest development in our ongoing restructuring, demonstrating our efforts to resize our manufacturing footprint,” said Michael Finley, senior vice president of fab operations. “We believe Macquarie’s extensive experience in the marketing and disposition of assets from advanced equipment to complete facilities positions them well to oversee the sale of Fab 2.”

data.title: Windtree shares rise as it announces agreement to become sourcing partner for Evofem
data.description:
• Windtree Therapeutics (NASDAQ:WINT) said on Thursday that it has entered into a license and supply agreement to become the sourcing partner for Evofem Biosciences (OTCQB:EVFM) for one of Evofem’s FDA-approved products -- PHEXXI.
  • The product is a hormone-free, on-demand prescription contraceptive vaginal gel that women control. PHEXXI annual revenues have grown to more than $19M, with more than 96,000 boxes of 12 pre-filled applicators sold in full year 2024.
  • Windtree has manufacturing contacts that it believes are capable of reducing pharmaceutical product cost of goods for PHEXXI.
  • Windtree will serve as the sourcing partner to Evofem and aims to help generate profitable revenue by contracting with the manufacturer to produce PHEXXI at a cost that is significantly below current levels.
  • Evofem will maintain ownership of the asset and continue to commercialize PHEXXI in the United States and internationally through strategic partnerships.
  • Windtree Therapeutics (WINT) shares were up more than 39% in premarket trading.

data.title: Founder Group secures $2.6M contract to build roof-top solar facility in Malaysia
data.description:
• Founder Group (NASDAQ:FGL) on Thursday said that it has secured a contract worth RM11.6M (about $2.6M) and partnered with a prominent solar installation company in Malaysia.
  • The company will serve as a contractor, responsible for the design, engineering, procurement, supply, delivery, construction and commissioning of a roof-top solar PV generating facility with nominal capacity.
  • This partnership will be a construction contract that is expected to be completed in 2 years. Upon completion of the facility construction, the company will sign an operations and maintenance agreement which will provide benefits over the next 15 to 21 years.
  • FGL -3.97% premarket to $1.4308
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Air India is said to be weighing major order for widebody planes
data.description:
Air India is in talks for a multi-billion-dollar order of widebody aircraft from both Boeing (NYSE:BA) and Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY), Reuters reported Thursday, citing industry sources. 

The order under consideration could include between 30 and 40 aircraft, with models such as the Airbus A350 and Boeing 777X being evaluated. The final deal might surpass 50 planes, but the details remain under discussion and have yet to be finalized.

More details about the deal may emerge as the Paris Air Show in June approaches.

Air India declined to comment to Reuters on market speculation, while Boeing (NYSE:BA) and Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) also refrained from making any statements regarding the potential order.

The possible order would build upon Air India’s ambitious fleet expansion efforts. In 2023, the airline placed a historic order for 470 aircraft from both manufacturers, followed by an additional purchase of 100 Airbus jets last year—primarily focusing on single-aisle models. The introduction of more widebody aircraft could further Air India’s modernization strategy as it seeks to regain its position in the global aviation market.

Currently, Air India has outstanding orders for 50 Airbus A350s, 10 Boeing 777Xs, and 20 Boeing 787 Dreamliners across its recent deals.

The decision to pursue further expansion comes as international air travel from India experiences a strong upward trend. Passenger traffic on international routes is projected to grow by 15-20% in the fiscal year ending March 31, significantly outpacing the 7-10% growth forecasted for domestic travel, according to Moody’s local ratings agency, ICRA.

As airlines worldwide scramble to secure production slots for widebody aircraft, the demand has intensified. This is partly due to the growing dominance of narrowbody aircraft orders, particularly from low-cost carriers such as IndiGo, India’s largest airline.

Air India’s transformation under Tata Group is being closely monitored by industry stakeholders after years of challenges under government ownership. However, progress has been hampered by ongoing delays in aircraft deliveries. The airline, which once had a reputation for world-class service, suffered from financial struggles, an ageing fleet, and deteriorating service standards since the mid-2000s.

Earlier this week, Air India CEO Campbell Wilson said global aircraft supply constraints are expected to persist for at least four more years due to shortages in engines, premium cabin seats, and certain fuselage components. These disruptions have led the airline to continue operating older aircraft, extending Tata’s five-year overhaul plan for the carrier.

Air India is not the only Indian airline facing challenges with aircraft deliveries. Akasa Air, the country’s newest carrier, is also grappling with shortages, which have left pilots without sufficient aircraft to operate, according to a recent Reuters report.

Despite these setbacks, Air India is determined to reclaim market share from international competitors such as Emirates, Lufthansa, and Turkish Airlines, which have traditionally attracted Indian travelers with modern fleets and superior service offerings. Currently, Indian airlines account for about 43-44% of outbound international passenger traffic, with foreign carriers still holding a larger share, according to ICRA.

Air India, along with its low-cost subsidiary Air India Express, is expected to receive around 20 new narrowbody and widebody aircraft this year, based on insights from U.K.-based consultancy Cirium Ascend.

data.title: ZEEKR Intelligent narrows its quarterly loss as deliveries ramp up
data.description:
ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holdings (NYSE:ZK) showed signs of moving closer to profitability in Q4 as the Chinese electric vehicle maker continues to scale up.

Total vehicle deliveries were up 99.8% year-over-year during the quarter to 79,250 units. Total vehicle deliveries for the full year were 87.2% higher to 222,123 units.

The company reported gross margin shot up to 19.0% for the quarter, compared with 14.2% a year ago and 16.0% in Q3 of 2024. Notably, ZEEKR (NYSE:ZK) narrowed its loss from operations by more than 50% from a year ago.

For the current quarter, the Zeekr (ZK) brand delivered 11,942 vehicles. Following the completion of the Lynk & Co acquisition, Zeekr (ZK) achieved total deliveries of 31,277 vehicles in February. This included 14,039 Zeekr (ZK) brand vehicles and 17,238 Lynk & Co brand vehicles.

"Looking ahead to 2025, we will continue expanding our product lineup and enhancing competitiveness. By leveraging AI-driven innovation and accelerating our global expansion strategy, we will advance our strategic vision and unlock greater synergies," highlighted CEO Andy An. "We remain committed to leading the premium new energy market through scalable growth and robust risk resilience," he added.

Shares of ZEEKR (ZK) fell 1.65% in premarket trading. The EV stock was up more than 15% into the Q4 earnings report.

data.title: Eli Lilly launches weight loss drug in India beating Novo Nordisk
data.description:
Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has launched its popular weight loss/diabetes therapy Mounjaro in India, beating its biggest rival, Novo Nordisk (NVO), in a market where obesity rates are fast rising, according to a statement from the U.S. drugmaker on Thursday.

The dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor co-agonist, also known as tirzepatide, has been made available in single-dose vials following the approval of India’s Central Drugs Standard Control Organization, the Indiana-based pharma giant said.

"The dual burden of obesity and type 2 diabetes is rapidly emerging as a major public health challenge in India,” The Times of India reported, quoting Winselow Tucker, president & GM, Lilly India, as saying. "Lilly is committed to collaborating with the government and industry to promote awareness and improve the prevention and management of these diseases.”

The company has introduced Mounjaro 2.5 mg and 5 mg vials at 3,500 rupees ($40.50) and 4,375 rupees ($50.63), respectively.

Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NYSE:LLY), which operates a duopoly in the weight loss drug market with Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), has yet to announce a timeline for its GLP-1 injectable Wegovy’s launch in India, where obesity rates are fast rising.

A government-run survey in 2019-2021 indicated that 24% of Indian women and about 23% of Indian men aged 15-49 were overweight or obese, compared to ~21% and ~19% in 2015-2019, respectively.

data.title: Shoe Carnival sets FY25 guidance below expectations on costs tied to growth strategy
data.description:
Shares of Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) are heading lower in Thursday’s early trading as the company set its FY25 guidance below Wall Street’s expectations, largely reflecting investment in its new growth strategy. The disappointing guidance overshadowed mixed fourth quarter results and the company’s plan to expand its Shoe Station business to capitalize on its popularity in the Southeast and as the footwear industry’s fastest growing retailer.

Over the next 24 months, Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) will rebanner 175 stores to the Shoe Station banner, which will bring the number of Shoe Station stores to 51% of the company’s present store fleet. This is expected to result in “significant” market share growth in areas where the company’s Shoe Carnival stores have underperformed. A future phase of the expansion plan includes new Shoe Station stores where the company does not compete.

“This rebanner strategy is expected to create significant financial leverage from a more productive store base,” the company said in a statement, adding that a market test where Shoe Carnival stores were closed, and new Shoe Station stores were opened showed strong customer response with a 10% higher profit contribution at new Shoe Station stores versus Shoe Carnival.

The first-year investment is forecasted to decrease FY25 operating income by between $20M to $25M, with a corresponding $0.65 decline in FY25 EPS.

To that end, Shoe Carnival (NASDAQ:SCVL) expects FY25 non-adjusted EPS of $1.60 to $2.10, below the $2.81 consensus estimate. Net sales are projected to be between $1.15B and $1.23B, also below $1.26B estimates.

For the fourth quarter, Shoe Carnival (SCVL) earned a profit of $0.54 per share, down 5 cents from a year ago but 10 cents better than expected. Total revenue decreased by 6.2% to $262.9M, $13M below expectations.
